perturbation.original_text: 1.1 For the purposes of this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms shall have the respective meanings set forth below and grammatical variations of such terms shall have corresponding meanings: (a) "Action" means any legal action, suit, claim, investigation, hearing or proceeding, including any audit, claim or assessment for Taxes or otherwise. (b) "Agreement" means this Joint Venture Agreement, dated December 19, 2019. (c) "Company" means the Joint Venture entity which will be registered and incorporated in a Canadian jurisdiction with its operating name as Novo Earth Therapeutics Inc. (d) "Cost" means cost of goods sold as defined in the financials of the Primary Project. (e) "Effective Date" is the date of the most recent final signature on this Agreement. (f) "EPC" means engineering, procurement, construction contracts. (g) "HFG" means Harvest Gold Farms Inc. (h) "Joint Venture" means a business arrangement where NVOS and HGF have agreed to pool their resources for the purpose of the Primary Project. (i) "Law" means any domestic or foreign, federal, state, provincial, municipal or local law, statute, ordinance, code, rule, or regulation having the force of law. (j) "NHL" means Novo Healthnet Limited. (k) "NVOS" means Novo Integrated Sciences Inc. (l) "Parties" means collectively, Harvest Gold Farms Inc. and Novo Integrated Sciences Inc. (m) "Party" identifies, separately, either Harvest Gold Farms Inc. or Novo Integrated Sciences Inc. (n) "Primary Contract" means the terms set out in this agreement for the Primary Project. (o) "Primary Project" means this agreement that outlines the development, management and arranging of medicinal farming projects involving hemp and cannabis cash crops. (p) "Tax(es)" means any federal, state, provincial, local or foreign tax, charge, fee, levy, custom, duty, deficiency, or other assessment of any kind or nature imposed by any Taxing Authority (including any income (net or gross), gross receipts, profits, windfall profit, sales, use, goods and services, ad valorem, franchise, license, withholding, employment, social security, workers compensation, unemployment compensation, employment, payroll, transfer, excise, import, real property, personal property, intangible property, occupancy, recording, minimum, alternative minimum, environmental or estimated tax), including any liability therefor as a transferee (including under Section 6901 of the Code or similar provision of applicable Law) or successor, as a result of Treasury Regulation Section 1.1502-6 or similar provision of applicable Law or as a result of any Tax sharing, indemnification or similar agreement, together with any interest, penalty, additions to tax or additional amount imposed with respect thereto. (q) "Taxing Authority" means the Internal Revenue Service, the Canada Revenue Agency and any other Authority responsible for the collection, assessment or imposition of any Tax or the administration of any Law relating to any Tax. (r) "Tax Return" means any return, information return, declaration, claim for refund or credit, report or any similar statement, and any amendment thereto, including any attached schedule and supporting information, whether on a separate, consolidated, combined, unitary or other basis, that is filed or required to be filed with any Taxing Authority in connection with the determination, assessment, collection or payment of a Tax or the administration of any Law relating to any Tax.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 For the purposes of this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms shall have the respective meanings set forth below and grammatical variations of such terms shall have corresponding meanings: (a) "Action" means any legal action. (b) "Agreement" means this Joint Venture Agreement. (c) "Company" means the Joint Venture entity. (d) "Cost" means project expenses. (e) "Effective Date" is the start date of this Agreement. (f) "EPC" means contract. (g) "HFG" means Harvest Farms Inc. (h) "Joint Venture" means a business arrangement. (i) "Law" means any law. (j) "NHL" means Novo Limited. (k) "NVOS" means Novo Inc. (l) "Parties" means collectively, Harvest Farms Inc. and Novo Inc.. (m) "Party" identifies, separately, either Harvest Farms Inc. or Novo Inc.. (n) "Primary Contract" means the terms set out in this document. (o) "Primary Project" means this document. (p) "Tax(es)" means any local tax. (q) "Taxing Authority" means the Revenue Agency. (r) "Tax Return" means any tax return.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definitions in Section 1.1, we create a contradiction with other sections that rely on the original, more specific definitions. For example, altering the definition of 'Tax(es)' to 'any local tax' contradicts the comprehensive list of tax types described in the original definition. This introduces ambiguity and potential disputes regarding tax obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16.1 The Company will ensure timely remittance of all tax liabilities and ensure specific adherence to any specific tax considerations. HGF will ensure maximum tax reduction and where possible elimination of any tax consideration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definitions of key terms like 'Tax(es)' and 'Taxing Authority.' The original text defines 'Tax(es)' as encompassing a wide range of federal, state, provincial, local, and foreign taxes, while the changed text limits it to 'any local tax.' Similarly, 'Taxing Authority' is changed from including the Internal Revenue Service and Canada Revenue Agency to simply 'the Revenue Agency.' This creates a contradiction with section 16.1, which states that 'The Company will ensure timely remittance of all tax liabilities and ensure specific adherence to any specific tax considerations.' By narrowing the definitions, the changed text implies a much smaller scope of tax liabilities, potentially leading the Company to not remit taxes beyond the 'local tax' as defined by the changed text, thus contradicting its obligation in section 16.1 to remit 'all tax liabilities.'

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of income on an annual basis.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specificity of 'thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis' creates an in-text contradiction. While NVOS is still obligated to remunerate HGF, the lack of a defined percentage makes the term unenforceable and contradicts any expectation of a fixed share as originally intended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific percentage (30%) of net company income that HGF is entitled to, rendering the remuneration term unenforceable due to a lack of clarity. This contradicts section 12.1, which explicitly states that HGF will receive thirty percent (30%) of net profit. The removal of the percentage creates a direct conflict regarding the distribution of profits, as the changed text provides no basis for calculating HGF's share, violating the conditions set forth in clause 12.1 and creating an Omissions-In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.6 To provide a minimum of seven thousand (7000) acres for the Primary Project to be identified by each individual lot, including size, and its placement in the annual rotation as per SCHEDULE A.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.6 To provide acres for the Primary Project.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the acre specifics and the reference to 'SCHEDULE A', HGF's obligation becomes vague. While they must still provide land, there's no longer a minimum acreage requirement or a defined list of lots. This directly contradicts the intent of the agreement to ensure a certain acreage, making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Acreage Identification for the Primary Project Disclosed in certificate of Robert Mattacchione, dated December 18, 2019.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific acreage requirement ('minimum of seven thousand (7000) acres') and the reference to SCHEDULE A, which defined the specific lots. The contradicted text, 'Acreage Identification for the Primary Project Disclosed in certificate of Robert Mattacchione, dated December 18, 2019,' assumes that a specific acreage and identification method has been previously defined. Without the acreage requirement and reference to SCHEDULE A in the modified section 10.6, there is no longer a clear and enforceable method for acreage identification, contradicting the assumption in the contradicted text.
